summary: The report is technically rigorous, data-grounded, and complete with all required sections, formulas, figures, and quantitative comparisons. It correctly interprets the NMSE/complexity trade-offs and flags the key DFT high-SNR crossover anomaly without overstating conclusions. Overall, the analysis is trustworthy and well aligned with the notebook evidence.
technical_strengths: Comprehensive mathematical formulation with clear symbol definitions and physically meaningful interpretations
Faithful integration of all required estimators and exact stepwise formulas
Strong evidence support via full SNR curves, confidence intervals, runtime, FLOP proxies, and sensitivity analyses
Balanced performance assessment including anomaly detection and method-wise strengths/weaknesses
technical_risks: LMMSE conclusions assume accurate PDP/covariance and may degrade under mismatch
DFT-truncation performance is sensitive to chosen truncation length and can invert rankings at high SNR
Synthetic-only setup omits synchronization and hardware impairments that affect field performance

main_concerns: DFT-truncated method shows high-SNR crossover due to truncation bias with default Lt=8
Spline interpolation underperforms linear baseline across this configuration

actionable_notes: Add covariance mismatch experiments to quantify LMMSE robustness envelope
Tune or adapt Lt per SNR/channel condition to prevent DFT high-SNR degradation
Include real-impairment stress tests (CFO, phase noise, timing errors) for external validity
